content.problemStatement:
Hermione, Harry, and Ron are at the entrance of the locked door in the flying key room. Of course, they try the age-old spell "Alohomora," but the door won't open. To open the door, they must use the key to the lock.

The lock consists of nnn disks. Each disk shows a digit from 000 to 999. Currently, the ithi^{\text{th}}ith disk shows digit sis_isi‚Äã, making a string s=s1s2‚Ä¶sns = s_1 s_2 \ldots s_ns=s1‚Äãs2‚Äã‚Ä¶sn‚Äã. When a disk in the lock shows iii, it can be rotated clockwise in time aia_iai‚Äã seconds to show (i+1)%10(i + 1) \% 10(i+1)%10, where %\%% denotes the modulo operator. You can rotate any disk of the lock clockwise any number of times.

Similarly, the key consists of m‚â§nm \leq nm‚â§n disks. Each disk shows a digit from 000 to 999. Currently, the ithi^{\text{th}}ith disk shows digit tit_iti‚Äã, making a string t=t1t2‚Ä¶tmt = t_1 t_2 \ldots t_mt=t1‚Äãt2‚Äã‚Ä¶tm‚Äã. When a disk in the key shows iii, it can be rotated clockwise in time bib_ibi‚Äã seconds to show (i+1)%10(i + 1) \% 10(i+1)%10. You can rotate any disk of the key clockwise any number of times.

The door opens when the string represented by the key is a substring of the string represented by the lock.

Find the minimum time (in seconds) in which they can unlock the door.

Please note that they **can't** rotate two disks (from the set of n+mn + mn+m disks) simultaneously.


Input Format

The first line contains two integers, nnn and mmm, denoting the length of strings sss and ttt respectively.
The second line contains the string sss, the lock string.
The third line contains the string ttt, the key string.
The fourth line contains 10 space-separated integers denoting a0,a1,‚ãØ‚Äâ,a9a_0, a_1, \cdots, a_9a0‚Äã,a1‚Äã,‚ãØ,a9‚Äã.
The fifth line contains 10 space-separated integers denoting b0,b1,‚ãØ‚Äâ,b9b_0, b_1, \cdots, b_9b0‚Äã,b1‚Äã,‚ãØ,b9‚Äã.


Output Format
Output a single integer denoting the minimum time (in seconds) in which the door can be opened.
constraints: Both sss and ttt consist of digits from 000 to 999.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example 111: Don't rotate any of the disks in the lock string. In the key string, we can rotate the disk two times, 8‚Üí98 \rightarrow 98‚Üí9 in time b8=2b_8 = 2b8‚Äã=2 seconds, and then 9‚Üí09 \rightarrow 09‚Üí0 in time b9=2b_9 = 2b9‚Äã=2 seconds to get a string 0, which is a substring of the string 60.

Explanation:
Example 222: In the lock, rotate the second disk once in time 333 seconds and the fourth disk once in time 111 second. In the key, rotate the second disk once in time 222 seconds. The lock string becomes 6253, and the key string becomes 253, which is a substring of the lock string. The total time taken is 3+1+2=63 + 1 + 2 = 63+1+2=6 seconds.
